events.event_description: The American Legation in Mexico City was established when Joel Robert Poinsett presented his credentials as Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary.

evidence.quote: The American Legation in Mexico was established on June 1, 1825, when Joel Robert Poinsett presented his credentials as Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary to President Guadalupe Victoria.

events.event_description: Mexico severed diplomatic relations with the United States in response to U.S. moves toward annexing Texas, handing U.S. Minister Wilson Shannon his dismissal.

evidence.quote: the Mexican Minister of Foreign Affairs informed U.S. Minister to Mexico, Wilson Shannon, on March 28, 1845, that Mexico was severing diplomatic relations with the United States.

events.event_description: Diplomatic relations were reestablished following the Treaty of Guadalupe-Hidalgo, restoring the U.S. Legation in Mexico.

evidence.quote: Diplomatic relations were reestablished on December 4, 1848, when U.S. President James K. Polk accepted Luis de la Rosa's credentials as Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary to the United States.

events.event_description: The U.S. suspended and then severed relations with Mexico's conservative government; Envoy Forsyth was recalled by President Buchanan.

evidence.quote: Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary to Mexico John Forsyth "suspended" relations, on June 21, 1858, between the legation and the conservative government of Miguel Miramón y Tarelo... Buchanan assented and recalled Forsyth on July 15, 1858.

events.event_description: Diplomatic relations were reestablished when Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary Robert M. McLane presented his credentials to President Benito Juárez.

evidence.quote: Diplomatic relations were reestablished on April 6, 1859, when Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary to Mexico, Robert M. McLane, presented his credentials to President Benito Juárez.

events.event_description: The American Legation in Mexico City was elevated to Embassy status when Powell Clayton presented his credentials as Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary.

evidence.quote: The Legation in Mexico was raised to Embassy status on January 3, 1899, when Powell Clayton presented his credentials as Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary to President Porfirio Díaz.

events.event_description: Mexico severed diplomatic relations with the United States following the U.S. occupation of Veracruz, handing Chargé d'Affaires O'Shaughnessy his passport and requesting his departure.

evidence.quote: Mexican officials severed diplomatic relations with the United States on April 22, when they handed Chargé d'Affaires ad interim O'Shaughnessy his passport and requested his departure from Mexico.

events.event_description: Diplomatic relations were reestablished at the embassy level when U.S. Ambassador Henry P. Fletcher presented his credentials to President Carranza.

evidence.quote: Diplomatic relations were reestablished on March 3, 1917, when U.S. Ambassador Henry P. Fletcher presented his credentials to President Carranza.
